topSignals.title: Top active signals

notice.closed: {{symbol}} closed at the latest market price.

positions.empty: No open positions. Fills land here; stops and targets exit them automatically.
positions.scaleOut: Scale out: close half the position at the latest closed price

orders.empty: No working orders. Limit and stop entries wait here until price reaches them, expire by their time-in-force, or are cancelled.

history.empty: No closed trades yet. Every exit — stop, target, trailing, or manual — is recorded here with its realized P&L and R multiple.

watchlist.title: Curated symbol monitor
watchlist.description: Grouped crypto and stock rows with sparkline, last price, active signal chips, alert access, and removal actions.
watchlist.notListed: Not listed yet — add it to the corridor as:

journal.loadError: Could not load the journal. Is the backend running?
journal.empty: No executed paper trades yet. Place a trade with a full plan — stop, target, risk, and reason — and it will appear here for review.
journal.notePlaceholder: What actually happened? What would you repeat — or never do again?
journal.footer: Tags feed the Review coach — honest tagging is what makes the recommendations yours.

missions.title: Missions
missions.tagline: Earned by conduct, not clicks — every criterion reads your real activity.
missions.loadError: Could not load missions. Is the backend running?
missions.noActive: No active missions. Open a category below and start one — active missions pin your next objective and link straight to where you act.

card.slotsFull: All active slots are taken — stand down from one first.

replay.title: Replay missions
replay.description: Stylized recreations of real market episodes. Play them bar by bar and make the calls — the debrief grades the decision, not the outcome.

missions.footer: Completing missions unlocks Academy levels. Community packs can add more via the extension SDK. Educational use only — not financial advice.

backtest.title: Statistically honest validation
backtest.description: Strategy selector, cost model inputs, in-sample vs out-of-sample metrics, and low-sample warnings. Parameter changes rerun the backend quant engine against stored corridor candles.
backtest.runFailed: The backend rerun failed - if data was just ingested, retry in a few seconds.
backtest.defaultRunMessage: Parameter changes rerun against the backend and update the metrics below.

status.running: Running backend backtest with the current cost model and validation split.
status.synced: Metrics below are synced from the backend quant engine using stored corridor candles.
status.noCandles: No stored {{timeframe}} candles for {{symbol}} — this symbol has {{available}} data. Pick one of those timeframes and rerun.

howTo.title: How this works
howTo.intro: It replays one setup's exact entry/stop/target rules over the candles stored on this machine and reports how that setup would have traded — win rate, expectancy in R, equity and drawdown — with fees and slippage charged on every round trip. It validates honestly: the sample is split chronologically into train/test so the headline numbers must survive data the rules never saw, and every run gets the workbench below — cost-stress scenarios, Monte Carlo drawdowns, bias gates, and the AI research review.
howTo.step1: Pick a preset — presets are generated from the engine's live signals (one per symbol/setup the engine currently detects) plus anything you saved earlier.
howTo.step2: Adjust fees, slippage, and the train/test split; the run re-executes instantly.
howTo.step3: Read the out-of-sample row and the workbench before trusting anything else.
howTo.step4Suffix: stores it under Settings → Strategies for re-running later.
howTo.footer: A backtest is evidence about the past, never a promise about the future. The matching Academy track (Backtesting & Statistical Honesty) teaches every number on this screen.

selector.title: Strategy / setup selector
selector.presetsHint: Detected-signal presets appear automatically once the engine has generated signals from stored market data. Saved strategies and the demo are always available above.
selector.composeHint: A strategy here = symbol × setup family × timeframe × costs × validation. Each family's entry/stop/target rules are the engine's published, backtested code (taught in Learn); new rule families come from extensions — never from a free-text box that can't be tested.

prefill.liveBacking: Live market corridor backing is available for {{symbol}} {{timeframe}} via {{provider}}, last updated {{time}} UTC.

empty.title: Start your first statistically honest backtest
empty.description: Choose a strategy source, confirm costs and the train/test split, then run it to see equity, drawdown, out-of-sample metrics, Monte Carlo, and the AI review.
empty.demoNote: The demo runs the built-in trend-pullback rules on stored market data — an educational sample, not a live signal.

error.title: Backtest results unavailable
error.description: The backtest results could not be loaded.

inOut.title: In-sample vs out-of-sample
inOut.overfittingWarning: Overfitting risk warning: out-of-sample win rate trails in-sample by {{gap}} percentage points.
inOut.noOverfitting: In-sample and out-of-sample performance remain close enough to avoid an overfitting warning.

sample.lowWarningBody: This setup has fewer than {{threshold}} trades, so the validation statistics should be treated as unstable.
sample.aboveThreshold: Sample size is above the configured minimum backtest threshold of {{threshold}} trades.

workbench.costStressNote: Same simulation, worse costs. An edge that dies at 2x slippage was never an edge.

settings.title: Provider, safety, AI, and strategy controls
settings.description: Simple/Advanced provider routing, masked API keys, paper/live safety controls, model selection, and saved strategies from the backtest screen.
settings.unavailableDescription: The settings surface could not be loaded.

risk.tradingModeDesc: Default is paper. Live mode is gated behind saved Alpaca credentials, explicit confirmation, and backend live-order safety checks.
risk.saveAlpacaFirst: Save Alpaca key ID and secret before enabling live mode
risk.liveEnabled: enabled for the current backend settings
risk.partialCandlesDesc: `act_on_partial_candles = false` is enforced in the current build. Signals use closed candles only.
risk.minSampleDesc: {{count}} trades. Strategies below this threshold show a warning badge in Backtesting.

legal.communityDesc: Licensed under AGPL-3.0-or-later. You may use, study, modify, and redistribute this software under the AGPL. Commercial licenses are available for proprietary embedding, closed-source redistribution, hosted products, and enterprise support.
legal.sourceDesc: The complete corresponding source for this build is available from the public repository.
legal.disclaimer: QuantGlass is research and decision-support software. It is not financial advice, an investment adviser, a broker-dealer, or a promise of trading performance.

keys.notifTestsHint: Notification tests use saved backend values for Telegram and email. Save changes first, then test each delivery path.
keys.providerSetupMapDesc: These are the same keyed providers shown in Advanced Providers. Save the listed keys below to move a provider from needs setup to configured.
keys.registryOfflineDetail: Provider registry metadata is unavailable while the backend is offline.
keys.sectionProvidersDesc: Market data and trading-provider credentials that affect registry availability and keyed transports.
keys.sectionAiDesc: Optional keys for OpenAI or OpenAI-compatible model routers. Local Ollama and LM Studio do not need a key by default.
keys.sectionTelegramDesc: Saved bot token and chat destination for Telegram alert delivery and test sends.
keys.sectionEmailDesc: SMTP host, authentication, sender, and recipients used for email alerts and test sends.
keys.keepBlankPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current saved key

strategies.libraryDesc: Where these come from: the Save strategy button on the Backtesting screen snapshots a run you liked — symbol, setup, timeframe, cost model, and validation split. Reopening Backtesting lists them as presets so you can re-run or tweak them later, and Import/Export moves them between machines as JSON. Deleting one only removes the saved preset, never any data.
strategies.emptyDesc: Use Backtesting → Save strategy to populate this tab or import a QuantGlass strategy JSON file.
strategies.selectOneFirst: Select at least one strategy before exporting.
strategies.exportedBrowser: Exported {{count}} selected strategies through browser download.
strategies.noValidStrategies: No valid strategies found in the selected file.
strategies.importFailed: Import failed because the selected file is not valid JSON.

extensions.registryDesc: Installed Python entry-point extensions can register providers, model gateways, indicators, strategies, and notification channels. External extension loading is off by default and only loads code you have explicitly installed.
extensions.loadedThisSession: Extensions are loaded in this session.
extensions.notLoadedThisSession: Extensions are not loaded in this session.
extensions.restartToApply: Restart QuantGlass to apply — the setting is read when the engine starts.
extensions.whereItem1: provider extensions join the data routing on the Providers tab; strategy extensions appear as setup presets on the Backtesting screen and feed the signal engine;
extensions.whereItem2: indicator extensions register alongside the built-in catalog below; lesson packs add tracks to the Academy (Learn) and mission packs add challenges to Missions — both as validated content that can never run code;
extensions.whereItem3: notification extensions add alert delivery channels. Two example packs ship in the repo's extensions/ folder to copy from.
extensions.loaderNoticePre: Extension code loading is off. Restart the backend with
extensions.loaderNoticePost: to discover trusted local packs and installed entry points.
extensions.emptyExtDesc: The backend did not return extension registry metadata.
extensions.registeredStrategiesDesc: Backtesting uses this registry. Extension strategies appear here and can contribute matching setup presets.
extensions.indicatorCatalogDesc: Computed indicators feed signals and backtests now. Catalog indicators are documented contribution targets for community packs and future chart overlays.
extensions.contributionSurfacesDesc: Backend-declared extension points that contributors can target with providers, execution adapters, notifications, import/export flows, data-quality checks, and UI panels.
extensions.emptySurfacesDesc: The backend did not return extension surface metadata.

providers.providerModeDesc: Choose between a simplified UI and explicit provider priority routing.
providers.usBuildNotice: US build defaults only expose US-compliant venues and data providers. Binance.com global, OKX, and Bybit are intentionally excluded; use Coinbase, Kraken, Gemini, Alpaca, Finnhub, and cached metadata providers in this build.
providers.cryptoRouteDesc: The app tries the primary exchange first, then secondary/fallback providers only when the previous route cannot return usable market data.
providers.stocksRouteDesc: Public providers work without keys. Keyed providers are used only after their credentials are saved.
providers.aiDesc: Configure local or API model gateways from the AI tab; deterministic narration remains the fallback.
providers.tradingDesc: Live execution is controlled from Risk & Safety and requires saved Alpaca credentials plus explicit confirmation.
providers.providerSetupDesc: Only selected keyed routes appear here. Use Advanced for full provider diagnostics.
providers.routesAvailable: Selected routes are available with the current credential setup.
providers.cryptoRoutingNote: Exchange-native candles and quotes come from the primary/fallback exchange. Broad discovery metadata stays on the secondary provider to match the cost-controlled adapter design.
providers.customProfilesDesc: Add provider identities and credentials for community adapters. Profiles appear in routing, but live market-data execution still requires an extension adapter for that provider.
providers.name: Name
providers.phName: OpenRouter market data, Internal feed, etc.
providers.phNotes: Adapter package, docs URL, supported symbols, rate-limit notes…
providers.adapterNote: Adapter: profile saved, extension adapter required for market-data execution.
providers.emptyCustomDesc: Add a provider profile to prepare credentials and routing for a community adapter.
providers.registryOffline: Registry metadata is unavailable while the backend is offline.

ai.statusAdapterRequiredDetail: This provider needs a native adapter extension before it can fetch models or run narration.
ai.statusBuiltInDetail: Always available as the deterministic fallback.
ai.statusNoKeyDetail: No saved API key is required by default.
ai.modelProvidersDesc: Connect local, cloud, router, and enterprise LLMs. Model lists are fetched from the selected provider when possible.
ai.baseUrlPlaceholder: https://api.openai.com/v1, http://127.0.0.1:11434, or any /v1 compatible endpoint
ai.noKeyHint: Local runtimes and public test gateways may not need a key.
ai.keepBlankPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current saved key
ai.timeoutHint: Use 60-120 seconds for cold-starting large local models.
ai.modelNarrationDesc: When on, the selected local or API model rewrites the summary. Failed calls and failed fact checks fall back to the deterministic template.
ai.tpl_template_desc: Offline deterministic narration fallback. No model call is made.
ai.tpl_ollama_desc: Run open models locally with zero server cost.
ai.tpl_lm_studio_desc: On-device GGUF models through the LM Studio server.
ai.tpl_llamacpp_desc: Lightweight local inference server when launched with OpenAI-compatible API.
ai.tpl_anthropic_desc: Claude models via the native Messages API.
ai.tpl_openai_desc: GPT-class models and reasoning models from the OpenAI API.
ai.tpl_deepseek_desc: Cost-efficient chat and reasoning models through an OpenAI-compatible API.
ai.tpl_openrouter_desc: One API key for many hosted model providers.
ai.tpl_azure_desc: Azure-hosted OpenAI deployments. Configure the deployment endpoint and API version if needed.
ai.tpl_bedrock_desc: AWS-managed foundation models through signed Bedrock Runtime requests.
ai.tpl_vertex_desc: GCP-hosted Gemini publisher models through Vertex AI REST.

symbolDetail.unknownDescription: The symbol-detail route stays explicit when a symbol id is invalid instead of silently swapping to another instrument.
symbolDetail.description: Large chart with overlay toggles, deterministic signal context, backend corridor candles with backend resampling where possible, backend-served news, and direct routes to the signal drawer, alert modal, and prefilled backtest.
